title: woodsia glabella subsp. asplenioides
intro: Woodsia glabella subsp. asplenioides, also known as Woods' fern or Asplenioid woodsia, is a subspecies of the Woodsia glabella fern, a type of fern native to North America. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Woodsia glabella subsp. asplenioides include treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as being used as a diuretic and a treatment for kidney stones. Herbal preparations of this plant include tea infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the dried fronds in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
